words.meaning_en: To make something bad or unsatisfactory better

words.example_sentence_hi: एक मिलनसार व्यक्ति जहाँ भी जाता है दोस्त बना लेता है।

words.example_sentence_hi: वह नौकरी का प्रस्ताव स्वीकार करने और अपने गृहनगर में रहने के बीच डगमगाती रही।

one_word_sub.question: To make an already bad problem or situation worse

words.sentence: Delay in relief ______ the suffering of the flood victims.

words.example_sentence_hi: नैतिक पहेली ने समिति को हफ्तों तक बाँटे रखा।

words.meaning_en: Confirm or support a statement or theory with evidence
words.example_sentence: Several witnesses came forward to corroborate her account of events.

one_word_sub.question: To confirm or support an argument or statement with evidence

words.sentence: The scientific data ______ the hypothesis proposed by the research team.

words.meaning_en: Greed for money or possessions
words.example_sentence: His cupidity led him to embezzle funds from the charity.

one_word_sub.question: Extreme greed for wealth or material possessions

words.sentence: The politician's ______ was exposed when the audit revealed missing public funds.
words.example_sentence_hi: उसके लोभ ने उसे दान के धन का गबन करने पर मजबूर किया।

words.meaning_en: Hasty and therefore not thorough or detailed

one_word_sub.question: Done quickly and without attention to detail

words.sentence: The inspector gave the site only a ______ examination before issuing the clearance.

words.example_sentence: Years of broken promises made him cynical about political change.

one_word_sub.question: A person who believes people are always motivated by selfish reasons

words.sentence: The ______ journalist exposed the corruption that others had chosen to ignore.
words.example_sentence_hi: टूटे हुए वादों ने उसे राजनीतिक बदलाव के प्रति संशयवादी बना दिया।

words.meaning_en: Showing fearlessness and determination
words.example_sentence: The dauntless soldier continued fighting despite being outnumbered.

one_word_sub.question: A person who shows courage without fear

words.example_sentence_hi: निडर सैनिक संख्या में कम होने के बावजूद लड़ता रहा।

one_word_sub.question: Proper and dignified behaviour suited to a formal occasion

words.sentence: Members of parliament are expected to maintain ______ during debates.
words.example_sentence_hi: दर्शकों ने पूरे गंभीर समारोह के दौरान शिष्टाचार बनाए रखा।

words.meaning_en: Humble submission and respect towards another person
words.example_sentence: He always spoke to elders with deference and respect.

one_word_sub.question: Respectful submission to the judgement or wishes of another

words.sentence: In ______ to the senior advocate, the junior lawyer remained silent during the hearing.
words.example_sentence_hi: वह हमेशा बड़ों से आदर और सम्मान के साथ बात करता था।

words.example_sentence: The demagogue manipulated the masses with inflammatory speeches.

one_word_sub.question: A leader who gains power by appealing to emotions and prejudices

words.sentence: The ______ used fear and propaganda to win the election.
words.example_sentence_hi: भड़काऊ नेता ने भड़काऊ भाषणों से जनता को हेरफेर किया।

words.meaning_en: Moral corruption; wickedness
words.example_sentence: The depravity of the regime shocked the international community.

one_word_sub.question: A state of extreme moral corruption or wickedness

words.sentence: The court heard shocking evidence of the accused's ______.

words.meaning_en: In low spirits from loss of hope or courage

one_word_sub.question: A person who has lost all hope and is in low spirits

words.example_sentence_hi: तीसरी बार परीक्षा में असफल होने के बाद वह निराश हो गई।

words.meaning_en: Intended to teach or give moral instruction
words.example_sentence: The novel has a didactic purpose, teaching readers about honesty.

words.example_sentence_hi: उपन्यास का एक शिक्षाप्रद उद्देश्य है, पाठकों को ईमानदारी के बारे में सिखाना।

words.meaning_en: Slow to act; causing delay

words.example_sentence_hi: विपक्ष की टालमटोल करने वाली रणनीति ने विधेयक को महीनों तक रोके रखा।

words.meaning_en: Perceive or recognize something
words.example_sentence: It takes wisdom to discern truth from propaganda.

one_word_sub.question: To perceive or recognize something that is not obvious

words.sentence: A good judge can ______ between genuine distress and false claims.
words.example_sentence_hi: सच्चाई को प्रचार से पहचानने के लिए बुद्धि की आवश्यकता होती है।

words.meaning_en: Careful and circumspect in one's speech or actions
words.example_sentence: A good secretary must be discreet about confidential matters.

one_word_sub.question: A person who is careful not to cause offence or reveal secrets

words.sentence: The doctor was ______ in discussing the patient's medical history.
words.example_sentence_hi: एक अच्छे सचिव को गोपनीय मामलों में विवेकशील होना चाहिए।

words.example_sentence: The draconian curfew banned all movement after 6 PM.

one_word_sub.question: Excessively harsh and strict laws or rules

words.example_sentence: His duplicity was exposed when the private messages were leaked.

words.example_sentence_hi: जब निजी संदेश लीक हुए तो उसकी धोखेबाजी उजागर हो गई।

words.meaning_en: Successful in producing a desired or intended result
words.example_sentence: The vaccine proved efficacious against all known variants of the virus.

words.example_sentence_hi: टीका वायरस के सभी ज्ञात वेरिएंट के विरुद्ध कारगर साबित हुई।

one_word_sub.question: A person who is famous and respected in their field

words.sentence: The award was presented by an ______ scientist who had contributed to space research.
words.example_sentence_hi: जाँच आयोग का नेतृत्व करने के लिए एक प्रतिष्ठित न्यायविद नियुक्त किया गया।

words.example_sentence: A good counsellor must have empathy for their clients' struggles.

one_word_sub.question: The ability to understand and share the feelings of others

words.sentence: The leader's ______ towards the flood victims was evident in his immediate response.
words.example_sentence_hi: एक अच्छे परामर्शदाता को अपने ग्राहकों के संघर्षों के प्रति सहानुभूति होनी चाहिए।

words.example_sentence: Malaria is endemic to several tropical regions.

words.example_sentence: Fame is often ephemeral — celebrated today and forgotten tomorrow.

words.example_sentence: An equitable distribution of resources is essential for social justice.

one_word_sub.question: Something that is fair and treats everyone equally

words.meaning_en: Tending to avoid commitment or direct answers
words.example_sentence: The witness gave evasive answers that frustrated the prosecutor.

one_word_sub.question: A person who avoids giving direct and clear answers

words.meaning_en: Requiring immediate action or attention; urgent
words.example_sentence: The exigent circumstances demanded the army be deployed immediately.

words.example_sentence: The exorbitant fees charged by private hospitals are unaffordable for most families.

words.meaning_en: Convenient and practical although possibly improper or immoral
words.example_sentence: The politician's decision was politically expedient but morally questionable.

words.sentence: Accepting the compromise was ______ but not entirely honest.

words.example_sentence: The lawyer exposed the fallacious reasoning in the opposing argument.

one_word_sub.question: An argument or belief that is based on false or mistaken reasoning

words.meaning_en: Having or displaying a passionate intensity
words.example_sentence: She was a fervent supporter of environmental conservation.

one_word_sub.question: A person who is extremely passionate and enthusiastic about something

words.example_sentence_hi: वह पर्यावरण संरक्षण की उत्साही समर्थक थी।

words.meaning_en: Sparing or economical with money or resources
words.example_sentence: Despite being wealthy, he lived a frugal lifestyle.

one_word_sub.question: A person who is economical and avoids wasteful spending

words.example_sentence_hi: धनी होने के बावजूद वह मितव्ययी जीवनशैली जीता था।

words.example_sentence: His grandiloquent speech impressed nobody because it lacked substance.

words.sentence: The minister's ______ address was ridiculed for being all words and no action.
words.example_sentence_hi: उनका अलंकारपूर्ण भाषण किसी को प्रभावित नहीं कर पाया क्योंकि उसमें सार नहीं था।

words.meaning_en: Fond of company; sociable
words.example_sentence: The gregarious student quickly made friends in the new school.

one_word_sub.question: A person who enjoys the company of others and is very sociable

words.example_sentence_hi: मिलनसार छात्र ने नए स्कूल में जल्दी दोस्त बना लिए।

words.meaning_en: Sly or cunning intelligence
words.example_sentence: She used guile rather than force to get what she wanted.

one_word_sub.question: Clever but deceitful behaviour used to achieve a goal

words.sentence: The diplomat used ______ to extract information without arousing suspicion.
words.example_sentence_hi: उसने जो चाहा वह पाने के लिए शक्ति की बजाय चालाकी का इस्तेमाल किया।

words.meaning_en: Hinder or impede the movement or progress of
words.example_sentence: The heavy rains hampered the rescue operations in the flood-affected areas.

one_word_sub.question: To obstruct or make progress difficult

words.sentence: Lack of funds continues to ______ the government's rural development plans.
words.example_sentence_hi: भारी बारिश ने बाढ़ प्रभावित क्षेत्रों में बचाव कार्य में बाधा डाली।

words.meaning_en: Leadership or dominance of one country or group over others
words.example_sentence: The nation's economic hegemony was challenged by emerging powers.

one_word_sub.question: Political or cultural dominance exercised by one group over others

words.sentence: The colonial era was defined by European ______ over vast parts of the world.
words.example_sentence_hi: राष्ट्र की आर्थिक प्रभुत्व को उभरती शक्तियों ने चुनौती दी।

words.example_sentence: He was exposed as a hypocrite who preached honesty but cheated on his taxes.

one_word_sub.question: A person who pretends to be virtuous but acts against those virtues

words.sentence: The self-proclaimed reformer was branded a ______ when his corrupt dealings were exposed.
words.example_sentence_hi: वह एक पाखंडी के रूप में उजागर हुआ जो ईमानदारी का उपदेश देता था लेकिन करों में धोखा देता था।

words.meaning_en: A person who attacks cherished beliefs or institutions
words.example_sentence: The scientist was an iconoclast who questioned accepted theories.

one_word_sub.question: A person who challenges established beliefs and traditions

words.example_sentence_hi: वैज्ञानिक एक परंपरा-विरोधी था जो स्वीकृत सिद्धांतों पर सवाल उठाता था।

words.meaning_en: Acting or done quickly and without thought or care
words.example_sentence: His impetuous decision to resign cost him his career.

one_word_sub.question: A person who acts hastily and without careful thought

words.sentence: An ______ leader often makes decisions that cause long-term problems.
words.example_sentence_hi: इस्तीफे के उसके जल्दबाज़ फैसले ने उसका करियर बर्बाद कर दिया।

words.meaning_en: Intelligently analytical and clear-thinking
words.example_sentence: Her incisive analysis of the data impressed the panel.

one_word_sub.question: Extremely sharp and clear in thinking or expression

words.sentence: The judge delivered an ______ verdict that set a new legal precedent.
words.example_sentence_hi: डेटा का उसका तीखा विश्लेषण पैनल को प्रभावित कर गया।

idioms.example_sentence: She was burning the midnight oil to finish her project before the deadline.

idioms.hindi: कठिन परिस्थिति को साहस से सहना
idioms.meaning_en: To endure a painful or difficult situation with courage
idioms.example_sentence: He decided to bite the bullet and confront his boss about the issue.

idioms.meaning_en: Something that seems bad at first but turns out to be good
idioms.example_sentence: Losing that job was a blessing in disguise — it led him to a better career.

idioms.meaning_en: Pursuing a mistaken or misguided course of action
idioms.example_sentence: If you think he's the culprit, you're barking up the wrong tree.

idioms.idiom: hit the nail on the head
idioms.meaning_en: To describe exactly what is causing a situation or problem
idioms.example_sentence: You hit the nail on the head when you said the team lacked motivation.
